sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: CC, Moody's: Ca, Fitch: CC

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

narrative.executive_summary: Ukraine's war entered its fifth year with mixed developments this week. The southern counteroffensive recaptured 300+ sq km in Zaporizhzhia Oblast -- the most significant territorial gains since 2023. Domestically-developed Flamingo cruise missiles struck Russia's Votkinsk ICBM factory 1,400 km from the border. The IMF approved an $8.1B four-year loan anchoring a $136.5B support package. However, Russia launched 1,500+ drones and 90+ missiles during the week. Sovereign credit at CC/Ca with 2,250 bps risk premium.
key_changes_this_week: Southern counteroffensive recaptured 300+ sq km in Zaporizhzhia Oblast -- most significant gains since 2023 (major)
Flamingo cruise missiles struck Votkinsk ICBM factory 1,400 km inside Russia (major)
IMF approved $8.1B 4-year loan; $1.5B disbursed immediately, anchoring $136.5B package (major)
IAEA brokered 4th ceasefire at Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant for power line repairs
Zelenskyy to announce wartime elections plan and peace deal referendum framework
Russia launched 1,500+ drones and 90+ missiles during the week
narrative.outlook: Ukraine's battlefield position improved meaningfully this week, strengthening negotiating leverage in ongoing trilateral peace talks (Abu Dhabi/Geneva). The Flamingo missile's successful deep strike capability represents a strategic shift in Ukraine's ability to target Russian military-industrial infrastructure. IMF lending provides critical fiscal stabilization. Key risks: Russian spring offensive planned for April-May targeting Slovyansk-Kramatorsk axis; US attention diverted by Iran conflict.
narrative.investor_implications: Ukraine sovereign bonds remain deeply distressed but the combination of IMF support, battlefield gains, and peace talk momentum provides tail-risk upside. Reconstruction-linked positions remain long-term plays contingent on ceasefire. European defense equities benefit from sustained conflict. Monitor peace talk developments closely.
narrative.data_quality_note: Ukraine macro data is wartime-quality. Territorial assessments rely on ISW and official Ukrainian sources. Russian casualty estimates (1.27M) are from Estonian intelligence and carry significant uncertainty bands.

active_alerts.title: Ukraine war enters 5th year; CC/Ca credit rating with 2,250 bps risk premium
active_alerts.description: Russia's war on Ukraine enters its 5th year with Russia occupying approximately 20% of Ukrainian territory. Ukraine's sovereign credit at CC/Ca/CC reflects extreme distress. Political risk premium at 2,250 bps. However, Ukraine's southern counteroffensive recaptured 300+ sq km in Zaporizhzhia Oblast and Flamingo cruise missiles struck Russian ICBM factory. IMF approved $8.1B 4-year loan anchoring $136.5B support package. The conflict remains the largest conventional war in Europe since WWII.

active_alerts.title: Ukraine counteroffensive gains: 300+ sq km recaptured; Flamingo missiles hit Russian ICBM factory
active_alerts.description: Ukraine's southern counteroffensive recaptured 300+ sq km in Zaporizhzhia Oblast. Flamingo cruise missiles struck Russia's Votkinsk ICBM factory 1,400 km inside Russia. IMF approved $8.1 billion 4-year loan anchoring $136.5 billion support package. These developments suggest improved Ukrainian military capability but the conflict remains far from resolution.
